<!-- prettier-ignore-start -->

# TS9007

**Category:** Typeclass Resolution · **Severity:** Error

## Message

```
summon type argument '{typeArg}' is not a registered typeclass
```

## Explanation

summon&lt;T&gt;() expects T to be a typeclass name that was registered with @typeclass.

Common typeclasses: Show, Eq, Ord, Clone, Hash, Default, Json

If you're using a custom typeclass, ensure it's decorated with @typeclass:
  @typeclass
  interface MyTypeclass&lt;A&gt; { ... }

<!-- prettier-ignore-end -->
<!-- generated:end -->

## Example

```typescript
import { summon } from "@typesugar/typeclass";

interface Pretty<A> {
  pretty(a: A): string;
}

interface Point {
  x: number;
  y: number;
}

const p = summon<Pretty<Point>>();
// error: TS9007 — summon type argument 'Pretty' is not a registered typeclass
```

## How to fix

`summon` only resolves interfaces that were registered as typeclasses. A plain generic interface isn't one — mark it with `/** @typeclass */`:

```typescript
import { summon } from "@typesugar/typeclass";

/** @typeclass */
interface Pretty<A> {
  pretty(a: A): string;
}

interface Point {
  x: number;
  y: number;
}

/** @impl Pretty<Point> */
const prettyPoint: Pretty<Point> = {
  pretty: (p) => `(${p.x}, ${p.y})`,
};

const p = summon<Pretty<Point>>();
```

The decorator form `@typeclass` on the interface also works, but the JSDoc form is portable (plain `tsc` flags decorators on interfaces with TS1206). If you meant a built-in typeclass like `Show`, `Eq`, or `Ord`, check the spelling and make sure it's imported from `@typesugar/std`.
